results.title: Fee Increase for Renewal Term
results._source_text_exact: 2.2 Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without requiring Contracting Party A's consent.
results.original_text: 2.2 Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without requiring Contracting Party A's consent.
results.reasoning_evidence: 2.2 Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without
results.quoted_text: Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without requiring Contracting Party A's consent.
results.explanation_simple: The Provider can increase fees by 15% without Contracting Party A consent.
results.why_it_matters: This clause allows the Provider to impose significant fee increases unilaterally, which could impact Contracting Party A's budget and financial planning.
results.legal_insight: This clause controls how the contract continues, renews, extends, or ends after the initial term.
results.market_comparison: This clause is relatively aggressive as it allows for a significant fee increase without Contracting Party A's agreement.
results.red_flag_reason: The unilateral fee increase poses a risk of unexpected financial burden on Contracting Party A.
results.negotiation_advice: Consider clarifying renewal mechanics, notice windows, automatic renewal, pricing changes, termination before renewal, and any service or payment changes.
section_path: 2. FEES AND PAYMENT

2.2 Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without requiring Contracting Party A's consent.
risk_reasons: Renewal provisions may affect contract duration, exit rights, or future pricing leverage.
Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.

legal_insight.text_before_validation: This clause controls how the contract continues, renews, extends, or ends after the initial term.

negotiation_advice.text_before_validation: Consider clarifying renewal mechanics, notice windows, automatic renewal, pricing changes, termination before renewal, and any service or payment changes.

results.title: Termination Rights
results._source_text_exact: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
results.original_text: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
results.reasoning_evidence: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Prov
results.quoted_text: Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
results.explanation_simple: The clause grants Provider unilateral termination rights while limiting Contracting Party A's termination rights.
results.why_it_matters: This imbalance can lead to operational risks for Contracting Party A, as they have less flexibility to terminate the agreement compared to Provider.
results.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
results.market_comparison: This clause reflects an aggressive termination structure favoring the Provider, as it allows for immediate termination without cause.
results.red_flag_reason: The clause creates a significant imbalance in termination rights, favoring Provider and exposing Contracting Party A to potential operational disruptions.
results.safer_alternative: Contracting Party A may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice.
section_path: 4. TERM AND TERMINATION

4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.

legal_insight.text_before_validation: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.

safer_alternative.text_before_validation: Contracting Party A may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice.

results.title: Term and Renewal
results._source_text_exact: 4.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides ninety (90) days' written notice of non-renewal.
results.original_text: 4.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides ninety (90) days' written notice of non-renewal.
results.reasoning_evidence: 4.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year te
results.quoted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides ninety (90) days' written notice of non-renewal.
results.explanation_simple: The clause establishes an automatic renewal process for the agreement.
results.why_it_matters: Automatic renewal can lead to extended obligations without active consent, potentially locking a party into terms they may wish to renegotiate.
results.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
results.red_flag_reason: The automatic renewal could lead to unintentional contract continuation, creating potential obligations without explicit consent.
results.recommendation: Review the termination triggers, notice requirements, cure periods, and post-termination obligations.
results.safer_alternative: The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, with renewal subject to mutual agreement at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the term.
results.negotiation_advice: Consider clarifying renewal mechanics, notice windows, automatic renewal, pricing changes, termination before renewal, and any service or payment changes.
section_path: 4. TERM AND TERMINATION

risk_reasons: Renewal provisions may affect contract duration, exit rights, or future pricing leverage.

safer_alternative.text_before_validation: The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, with renewal subject to mutual agreement at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the term.

results.title: Ownership of Deliverables
results._source_text_exact: 3.2 All Deliverables shall be deemed "work made for hire" and shall vest exclusively in Contracting Party A upon full payment of the applicable fees.
results.original_text: 3.2 All Deliverables shall be deemed "work made for hire" and shall vest exclusively in Contracting Party A upon full payment of the applicable fees.
results.reasoning_evidence: 3.2 All Deliverables shall be deemed "work made for hire" and shall vest exclusively in Contracting Party A upon full payment of the applicable fees.
results.quoted_text: 3.2 All Deliverables shall be deemed "work made for hire" and shall vest exclusively in Contracting Party A upon full payment of the applicable fees.
results.explanation_simple: The clause establishes that all Deliverables are considered 'work made for hire' and will belong to Contracting Party A after payment.
results.why_it_matters: This clause ensures that Contracting Party A retains ownership of the Deliverables, which is critical for protecting intellectual property rights.
results.legal_insight: Manual review required: generated text did not pass source-fidelity validation.
results.market_comparison: This clause is common in contracts involving creative work or services where ownership of the output is a key concern.
results.recommendation: Confirm ownership of pre-existing materials, newly created deliverables, license scope, usage restrictions, moral rights, and post-termination rights.
section_path: 3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

results.title: Conflict Resolution
results._source_text_exact: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
results.original_text: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific p
results.quoted_text: In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
results.explanation_simple: This clause establishes that the Agreement takes precedence over any conflicting terms in a Statement of Work (SOW) unless the SOW explicitly states otherwise.
results.why_it_matters: It clarifies which document governs in case of discrepancies, reducing potential disputes over conflicting terms.
results.legal_insight: This clause provides a clear hierarchy between the Agreement and SOW, which can help prevent confusion and litigation regarding conflicting obligations. However, it relies on the SOW to explicitly state any overrides, which could lead to ambiguity if not carefully drafted.
results.market_comparison: This is a common approach in contracts to establish precedence between documents.
results.recommendation: The clause effectively clarifies the governing document in case of conflict, which is a reasonable approach.
section_path: 1. SERVICES AND STATEMENTS OF WORK

recommendation.text_before_validation: The clause effectively clarifies the governing document in case of conflict, which is a reasonable approach.

results.title: Payment Terms
results._source_text_exact: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
results.original_text: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
results.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue intere
results.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
results.explanation_simple: The clause establishes payment terms and interest on late payments.
results.why_it_matters: It defines the timeline for payments and the consequences of late payments, which can affect cash flow.
results.market_comparison: The payment terms are relatively standard, but the interest rate on late payments may be considered aggressive depending on the industry.
section_path: 2. FEES AND PAYMENT
2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.

results.title: Retention of Rights
results._source_text_exact: 3.1 Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.
results.original_text: 3.1 Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.
results.reasoning_evidence: 3.1 Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.
results.quoted_text: Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.
results.explanation_simple: The Provider maintains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property.
results.why_it_matters: This clause clarifies that the Provider's tools and methodologies are not transferred to Contracting Party A, protecting the Provider's intellectual property rights.
results.market_comparison: This clause is common in service agreements, ensuring that service providers retain ownership of their intellectual property.
section_path: 3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
3.1 Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.

results.title: Limitation of Liability
results._source_text_exact: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
results.original_text: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
results.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN TH
results.quoted_text: EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.
results.explanation_simple: The clause limits the Provider's liability to the fees paid by Contracting Party A in the last three months, excluding certain breaches.
results.why_it_matters: This limitation caps the Provider's financial exposure, which could impact Contracting Party A's ability to recover full damages in case of a claim.
results.legal_insight: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.
results.market_comparison: This type of liability limitation is common in commercial agreements, but the exclusion of certain breaches may be seen as a protective measure for the Provider.
results.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
results.safer_alternative: Provider's liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim, except for breaches of confidentiality or indemnification obligations.
section_path: 5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.
Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.
Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.

legal_insight.text_before_validation: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.

recommendation.text_before_validation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.

safer_alternative.text_before_validation: Provider's liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim, except for breaches of confidentiality or indemnification obligations.

results.title: Dispute Resolution
results._source_text_exact: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
results.original_text: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. results.explanation_simple: The clause requires disputes to be resolved through negotiation and, if unsuccessful, through binding arbitration.
results.why_it_matters: This clause limits the parties' ability to pursue litigation, which may affect their legal options in case of a dispute.
results.legal_insight: The clause determines where and how disputes are resolved, so enforceability, cost, language, and practical access to the forum should be checked.
results.recommendation: Confirm that the chosen law and forum are practical, accessible, and consistent with the parties' location and transaction.
section_path: 6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. risk_reasons: Dispute resolution provisions determine how disagreements are resolved.

clauses.title: Conflict Resolution
clauses._source_text_exact: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
clauses.original_text: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 1.2 In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific p
clauses.quoted_text: In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and a SOW, this Agreement shall control unless the SOW expressly states an intent to override a specific provision.
clauses.explanation_simple: This clause establishes that the Agreement takes precedence over any conflicting terms in a Statement of Work (SOW) unless the SOW explicitly states otherwise.
clauses.why_it_matters: It clarifies which document governs in case of discrepancies, reducing potential disputes over conflicting terms.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause provides a clear hierarchy between the Agreement and SOW, which can help prevent confusion and litigation regarding conflicting obligations. However, it relies on the SOW to explicitly state any overrides, which could lead to ambiguity if not carefully drafted.
clauses.market_comparison: This is a common approach in contracts to establish precedence between documents.
clauses.recommendation: The clause effectively clarifies the governing document in case of conflict, which is a reasonable approach.

clauses.title: Payment Terms
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.

clauses.explanation_simple: The clause establishes payment terms and interest on late payments.
clauses.why_it_matters: It defines the timeline for payments and the consequences of late payments, which can affect cash flow.
clauses.market_comparison: The payment terms are relatively standard, but the interest rate on late payments may be considered aggressive depending on the industry.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN TH
clauses.quoted_text: EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause limits the Provider's liability to the fees paid by Contracting Party A in the last three months, excluding certain breaches.
clauses.why_it_matters: This limitation caps the Provider's financial exposure, which could impact Contracting Party A's ability to recover full damages in case of a claim.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.
clauses.market_comparison: This type of liability limitation is common in commercial agreements, but the exclusion of certain breaches may be seen as a protective measure for the Provider.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
clauses.safer_alternative: Provider's liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim, except for breaches of confidentiality or indemnification obligations.
section_path: 5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.
Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.
Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
clauses.original_text: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.

clauses.quoted_text: The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause requires disputes to be resolved through negotiation and, if unsuccessful, through binding arbitration.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause limits the parties' ability to pursue litigation, which may affect their legal options in case of a dispute.
clauses.legal_insight: The clause determines where and how disputes are resolved, so enforceability, cost, language, and practical access to the forum should be checked.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm that the chosen law and forum are practical, accessible, and consistent with the parties' location and transaction.
section_path: 6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
risk_reasons: Dispute resolution provisions determine how disagreements are resolved.

clauses.title: Fee Increase for Renewal Term

clauses.quoted_text: Provider reserves the right to unilaterally increase fees for any renewal term by up to fifteen percent (15%) upon thirty (30) days' written notice, without requiring Contracting Party A's consent.
clauses.explanation_simple: The Provider can increase fees by 15% without Contracting Party A consent.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause allows the Provider to impose significant fee increases unilaterally, which could impact Contracting Party A's budget and financial planning.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause controls how the contract continues, renews, extends, or ends after the initial term.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is relatively aggressive as it allows for a significant fee increase without Contracting Party A's agreement.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The unilateral fee increase poses a risk of unexpected financial burden on Contracting Party A.

clauses.title: Termination Rights
clauses._source_text_exact: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
clauses.original_text: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Prov
clauses.quoted_text: Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause grants Provider unilateral termination rights while limiting Contracting Party A's termination rights.
clauses.why_it_matters: This imbalance can lead to operational risks for Contracting Party A, as they have less flexibility to terminate the agreement compared to Provider.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause reflects an aggressive termination structure favoring the Provider, as it allows for immediate termination without cause.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The clause creates a significant imbalance in termination rights, favoring Provider and exposing Contracting Party A to potential operational disruptions.
clauses.safer_alternative: Contracting Party A may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice.
section_path: 4. TERM AND TERMINATION

4.2 Provider may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately, for any reason or no reason, upon written notice to Contracting Party A. Contracting Party A may terminate only for Provider's uncured material breach following sixty (60) days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.

clauses.title: Term and Renewal
clauses._source_text_exact: 4.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides ninety (90) days' written notice of non-renewal.

clauses.quoted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides ninety (90) days' written notice of non-renewal.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause establishes an automatic renewal process for the agreement.
clauses.why_it_matters: Automatic renewal can lead to extended obligations without active consent, potentially locking a party into terms they may wish to renegotiate.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause determines when the contract can end and whether the affected party has notice or an opportunity to cure.
clauses.red_flag_reason: The automatic renewal could lead to unintentional contract continuation, creating potential obligations without explicit consent.
clauses.recommendation: Review the termination triggers, notice requirements, cure periods, and post-termination obligations.
clauses.safer_alternative: The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of two (2) years, with renewal subject to mutual agreement at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the term.
clauses.negotiation_advice: Consider clarifying renewal mechanics, notice windows, automatic renewal, pricing changes, termination before renewal, and any service or payment changes.

clauses.why_it_matters: This clause clarifies that the Provider's tools and methodologies are not transferred to Contracting Party A, protecting the Provider's intellectual property rights.
clauses.market_comparison: This clause is common in service agreements, ensuring that service providers retain ownership of their intellectual property.
section_path: 3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
3.1 Provider retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing tools, methodologies, and know-how used to deliver the services.

clauses.title: Payment Terms
clauses._source_text_exact: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
clauses.original_text: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue intere
clauses.quoted_text: Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause establishes payment terms and interest on late payments.
clauses.why_it_matters: It defines the timeline for payments and the consequences of late payments, which can affect cash flow.
clauses.market_comparison: The payment terms are relatively standard, but the interest rate on late payments may be considered aggressive depending on the industry.
section_path: 2. FEES AND PAYMENT
2.1 Contracting Party A shall pay all fees set forth in each SOW within forty-five (45) days of the invoice date. Any amount unpaid after sixty (60) days shall accrue interest at 1% per month.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses._source_text_exact: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
clauses.original_text: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN TH
clauses.quoted_text: EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause limits the Provider's liability to the fees paid by Contracting Party A in the last three months, excluding certain breaches.
clauses.why_it_matters: This limitation caps the Provider's financial exposure, which could impact Contracting Party A's ability to recover full damages in case of a claim.
clauses.legal_insight: This clause establishes the core non-disclosure obligation: who is bound by it, what standard of care applies, and whether authorized sub-disclosure (e.g. to representatives) is permitted.
clauses.market_comparison: This type of liability limitation is common in commercial agreements, but the exclusion of certain breaches may be seen as a protective measure for the Provider.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm the obligation clearly names who it binds, requires at least equivalent protection for any authorized sub-disclosure, and states a concrete standard of care.
clauses.safer_alternative: Provider's liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim, except for breaches of confidentiality or indemnification obligations.
section_path: 5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

5.1 EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CLIENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.
risk_reasons: Liability allocation may affect financial exposure or available remedies.
Confidentiality obligations may continue beyond contract termination.
Payment provisions may affect cash flow or performance rights.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
clauses.original_text: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
clauses.reasoning_evidence: 6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, an
clauses.quoted_text: The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
clauses.explanation_simple: The clause requires disputes to be resolved through negotiation and, if unsuccessful, through binding arbitration.
clauses.why_it_matters: This clause limits the parties' ability to pursue litigation, which may affect their legal options in case of a dispute.
clauses.legal_insight: The clause determines where and how disputes are resolved, so enforceability, cost, language, and practical access to the forum should be checked.
clauses.recommendation: Confirm that the chosen law and forum are practical, accessible, and consistent with the parties' location and transaction.
section_path: 6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

6.1 The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation between senior executives for thirty (30) days. Failing resolution, any dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules, seated in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single arbitrator, in the English language.
risk_reasons: Dispute resolution provisions determine how disagreements are resolved.
